description: MtgoCard, MtgoDeck, MtgoStanding, MtgoEventData, FORMAT_MAP, and extractEventData are duplicated between src/scrapers/mtgo.ts and src/parsers/mtgo.ts. Export them from the scraper and import in the parser to eliminate the copy-paste divergence risk.
title: DRY MTGO types: export from scraper, import in parser
Phase 2 audit — test query pipeline end-to-end with real data
MTGA/MTGO deck list output formatting
Intent extraction — structured JSON from user query
Phase 3: Frontend MVP

description: parseJob() deletes deck_cards then re-inserts. If insert fails, deck is left with no cards and the job is marked failed, but the tournament/deck rows are already written — DB inconsistency. Needs a Postgres RPC for atomicity. Track as known limitation; fix in Phase 2 alongside archetype classifier.
title: Track: deck_cards delete+insert non-atomic (fix in Phase 2)
Chat UI — hybrid interface with persistent context filters
MTGTop8 scraper + parser

close_reason: render.yaml created with web service, scraper worker, 12h scrape cron, and weekly Scryfall sync cron. Env vars marked sync:false — must be set manually in Render dashboard.
title: Render cron wiring — schedule scrape worker every 12h
Archetype clustering pipeline — Jaccard + LLM labeling + card signature
Phase 3 audit — UX review, deck list format, full user flow

description: Domain firemind.gg was recently purchased. Steps: 1) In Render dashboard for firemind-web, go to Settings > Custom Domains and add firemind.gg. 2) Add the CNAME or A record Render provides to your DNS registrar. 3) Wait for DNS propagation (up to 48h). 4) Confirm Render shows the domain as verified and HTTPS cert is provisioned. 5) Test https://firemind.gg in browser.
title: Verify firemind.gg custom domain on Render
SQL retrieval layer — query tournaments/decks based on intent

description: newUrls.indexOf(url) called inside for...of loop in scrapeNewMtgoEvents() is O(n^2). Trivial fix: use for...of with entries() or a counter index.
title: Fix O(n^2) indexOf in scrape loop
MTGGoldfish scraper + parser
Embeddings + pgvector retrieval

close_reason: 77,649 cards upserted via streaming pipeline with oracle_id
title: Scryfall bulk sync
cluster.ts LLM rate limiting between archetype labels
LLM provider abstraction — LLMProvider interface + ClaudeProvider
Surface confidence in UI + LLM hedging language
Context assembly + query API route

description: inferTier() in src/parsers/mtgo.ts defaults to 'challenge' when no pattern matches. This silently misclassifies unknown events (Opens, Pro Tours, etc). Return null instead — the column is nullable and the check constraint allows it.
title: Fix inferTier to return null for unknown events

description: Migration: ALTER TABLE cards ADD COLUMN usd numeric, ADD COLUMN tix numeric. Update sync-scryfall.ts to include prices field in upsert. Scryfall bulk export has prices.usd and prices.tix on each card object.
title: Add usd/tix columns to cards table and backfill from Scryfall sync
Phase 1 audit — review schema, pipeline end-to-end, data integrity
Phase 4 audit — clustering accuracy, confidence scores, multi-source retrieval
Phase 1: Data Foundation

description:
Scryfall bulk export already includes usd and tix price fields per printing. Work:
1. Add usd and tix columns to the cards table (migration)
2. Include prices in sync-scryfall upsert payload
3. Add deck price computation to retrieval layer: sum (quantity * card price) across mainboard to get total deck cost
4. Surface in query responses: when user asks about budget or affordability, rank decks by price
5. Support both USD (paper) and tix (MTGO) pricing
6. Example query: 'what is the most affordable Modern deck that still wins consistently'
title: Price-aware queries: scrape and surface deck cost data

description: mindsculptor-scraper worker service in render.yaml restarts immediately after each run (Render default behavior). The cron job already handles 12h scheduling. Worker is redundant and would hammer MTGO continuously. Remove the worker service block from render.yaml.
title: Remove Render background worker service
snapshot fetchMetaShares should filter by method='jaccard' to avoid cross-method double counting
Phase 4: Make It Smart

close_reason: Plain fetch scraper built, 4 challenge events stored, idempotent
title: MTGO scraper

close_reason: MTGO parser complete. 4 challenge events parsed: 4 tournaments, ~120 decks, 2905 deck_cards. Stable IDs via SHA-256 hash. Placement from standings.rank. Known: MTGO decklists page only publishes mainboard — sideboard not in window.MTGO.decklists.data JSON. Will investigate .dek download format separately.
title: MTGO parser — normalize scrape_jobs into tournaments/decks/deck_cards
Next.js scaffold + Supabase client setup

description: Auth, payments, Firemind design system, /chat and /data UI sections, SEO foundations. This phase ships the product as designed in product/.
title: Phase 5: Product Layer

description: Wire up Supabase Auth with Discord and Google providers. No email/password. On success, upsert user row in public.users with tier='casual' default. Required before any feature gating can be enforced.
title: Discord OAuth + Google OAuth via Supabase Auth

description: Public SSR page, no auth required, fully crawlable. Main SEO surface. Shows: tier + meta share + win rate header; meta share over time line chart (30/90d); win rate over time line chart; recent MTGO results table (date, event, placement, pilot). Oracle CTAs: 'Ask the Firemind about this deck' (free, uses query quota) + 'Generate sample deck list' (Spike only, blurred for Casual) + 'Sideboard plan vs the field' (Spike only). Alert subscription bell (1 free / unlimited Spike). OG image: archetype name, meta%, trend arrow, format badge. See product/information-architecture.md and product/charts.md.
title: /data/[format]/[archetype] — archetype detail page

description: Dynamic OG images via @vercel/og for /data/[format] (format name + top 3 archetypes + share%) and /data/[format]/[archetype] (archetype name, meta%, trend arrow, format badge). Sitemap.xml covering all public /data pages, updated on each scrape cycle. Both are day-one SEO infrastructure for Reddit shares and Meta ad previews.
title: OG images + sitemap

description: Public permalink to a single oracle response (never a full conversation). Store shareable responses in oracle_results table: id, query, response, format, archetype_context, created_at. Page shows: query, response, context, timestamp. Social share buttons (Reddit, copy link). CTA: 'Get your own oracle queries — it is free'. Archetype names in the shared response link to /data/[format]/[archetype].
title: Shareable oracle result permalinks — /chat/results/[id]

description: Full audit before closing Phase 5. Check: auth flows work end-to-end (Discord + Google, both tiers); Stripe webhooks reliably update user tier; query rate limits enforced server-side and can't be bypassed; all /data pages SSR with real data and no auth wall; OG images render correctly; sitemap is complete; soft gates appear inline without hard redirects; /chat/results/[id] is public and shareable. Fix every issue found before closing.
title: Phase 5 audit — auth flows, gating, UI, SEO

description: Stripe subscription for Spike tier at $4.99/mo. Webhook from Stripe to Supabase: on subscription active/cancelled, update users.tier. Stripe billing portal link for self-serve management. Annual billing at ~20% discount.
title: Stripe subscription integration — Casual/Spike tiers

description: Server-side enforcement of Casual vs Spike limits. Oracle query rate limit: 5/day Casual, 30/day Spike (tracked in DB, reset daily). Chat history: Spike only. Deck list generation CTA: Spike only. Matchup matrix: Spike only. Alert subscriptions: 1 Casual, unlimited Spike. Soft inline gates in UI — never hard redirect.
title: Feature gating middleware — enforce tier limits

description: Implement the Izzet/dracogenius design system from product/design.md. Tailwind config with color tokens: bg #06080F, surface #0C1220, border #172035, primary #4F8EF7 (electric blue), secondary #D4552A (Izzet flame-red), copper #B87333, gold #C9A050, text #E4EEFF, muted #4A5878. Typography: Geist/Space Grotesk for display, Geist Mono for data. Blueprint schematic background texture (very low opacity). Electric-blue glow on interactive borders. Oracle output component: electric-blue left-border glow, copper Firemind label.
title: Firemind design system — tokens, typography, component primitives

description: Top nav: [⚡ Firemind logo] [Chat] [Data] [🕐 History][🔔 Alerts][avatar▾]. Format toggle only appears in /data section. Unauthenticated: hide icon tray, show [Sign in] [Go Spike]. All secondary views (history, alerts, archetype detail) open as drawers over the current page. See product/information-architecture.md.
title: Two-section layout + minimal nav

description: Public marketing page. Hero: headline 'Know what's winning. Before you register.' + full-width oracle input + 4 suggested query chips. Live metagame glimpse below fold (top 3 archetypes per format, real data, no auth). Social proof strip: events processed + queries answered. Pricing section: Casual vs Spike inline. CTAs: Discord/Google OAuth signup + 'See metagame charts'. All content in real HTML — indexable. See product/information-architecture.md.
title: Landing page — / route

description: Track daily oracle query counts per user in Supabase (oracle_queries table: user_id, date, count). Enforce 5/day Casual, 30/day Spike at the API route level before calling LLM. Return 429 with remaining count in response. Reset at midnight UTC. Unauthenticated users must sign in to query — no anonymous queries.
title: Oracle query rate limiting — server-side enforcement

description: Full-width chat interface at /chat. Empty state: centered prompt + 4 suggested query chips. Query counter inline with input ('5 / 5 queries today'). Oracle responses render inline data cards: archetype bars with meta%, trend arrows, archetype names as links to /data/[format]/[archetype]. Spike CTA when query limit hit. Unauthenticated: show interface, block query with sign-in prompt. See product/information-architecture.md for response format mockup.
title: /chat — full chat UI

description: Public SSR page, no auth required, fully crawlable. Charts: (1) meta share horizontal bar chart filterable by 7d/30d/90d, sortable by meta share or win rate; (2) top movers widget — biggest gainers/losers week over week; (3) meta share trend lines — multi-line chart, one per top archetype over time. Archetype rows link to /data/[format]/[archetype]. Oracle summary card at bottom with CTA to /chat. OG image: format name + top 3 archetypes + share%. See product/charts.md.
title: /data/[format] — format overview page
Metagame snapshots + confidence scoring

description: mtggoldfish.ts and mtgtop8.ts call resolveCardIds() inside the per-deck loop — one DB query per deck. mtgo.ts correctly batches all card names upfront before the loop. Fix both goldfish and top8 to collect all card names across all decks first, then do a single resolveCardIds call, and pass the pre-built map into each deck's row construction.
title: Parser N+1: batch card ID resolution in goldfish and top8 parsers

close_reason: Migration 20260301214507 + audit fixes 20260301215625 pushed and verified
title: Define Supabase schema

description: fetchHtml() in src/scrapers/mtgo.ts has no timeout. If mtgo.com hangs, the cron/worker hangs until Render kills the process. Add AbortController with 30s timeout.
title: Add fetch timeout to MTGO scraper

description: In retrieveContext, sum (quantity * card.usd) and (quantity * card.tix) across mainboard cards for each deck in top_decks. Add deck_cost_usd and deck_cost_tix to DeckSummary. Include in assembleContext output. Update RESPONSE_SYSTEM to instruct LLM to rank by price when user asks about affordability/budget. Example query: 'most affordable Modern deck that wins consistently'.
title: Compute deck cost in retrieval layer and surface in query responses
Implement in-memory query cache with 1hr TTL and 200-entry cap
Phase 2: AI Query Layer
